resources.name: scarleteel-secondary-user
resources.purpose: Zero-permission IAM user whose access keys are stored in terraform.tfstate — the lateral movement target
resources.configuration_notes: force_destroy=True. No attached policies — zero permissions by design.

resources.name: scarleteel-secondary-key
resources.purpose: Long-term IAM access key for the bait user. Stored in terraform.tfstate bait file.
resources.configuration_notes: Key ID and secret exported to terraform.tfstate bait object.

resources.name: scarleteel-vuln-ec2-role
resources.purpose: Over-privileged IAM role attached to the EC2 instance. Grants S3, Lambda, CloudTrail permissions that the attacker abuses after credential theft.
resources.configuration_notes: Inline policy: s3:ListAllMyBuckets/GetObject/ListBucket + 7 Lambda read APIs + cloudtrail:StopLogging/DescribeTrails. All on Resource: *. SSM core attached for debug access.

resources.name: scarleteel-dummy-logs
resources.purpose: Bait S3 bucket containing fake syslog data for exfiltration simulation.

resources.name: scarleteel-dummy-scripts
resources.purpose: Bait S3 bucket containing fake customer DB loader script for exfiltration simulation.

resources.name: scarleteel-tf-state
resources.purpose: S3 bucket containing terraform.tfstate bait file with plaintext IAM access keys for the zero-permission bait user.
resources.configuration_notes: force_destroy=True. Contains terraform.tfstate with aws_iam_access_key resource.

resources.name: ProprietaryAlgoFunc
resources.purpose: Lambda function with DB_PASS environment variable. The attacker exfiltrates the source code URL and reads env vars via ListVersionsByFunction.
resources.configuration_notes: Python 3.9 runtime. Environment variable DB_PASS=SuperSecretCustomerPass123. Minimal execution role.

resources.name: scarleteel-trail
resources.purpose: CloudTrail trail that the attacker disables via StopLogging in Phase 4.
resources.configuration_notes: Single-region trail. Logs to scarleteel-trail-logs S3 bucket. Requires bucket policy allowing cloudtrail.amazonaws.com service principal.

resources.name: scarleteel-vuln-container-host
resources.purpose: EC2 instance running a Docker container with a vulnerable Flask web application (command injection on /cmd endpoint). IMDSv1 enabled with hop_limit=2 for Docker bridge access.
resources.configuration_notes: Amazon Linux 2023 t3.micro. UserData installs Docker and builds/runs the Flask container. metadata_options: http_tokens=optional, http_endpoint=enabled, hop_limit=2. Security group: port 8080 open to 0.0.0.0/0.
